response_message.content: The Vagrantfile now defines three Ubuntu VMs using a loop, mirroring the existing Arch VM setup. This adds `ubuntu1`, `ubuntu2`, and `ubuntu3` with the specified box and provisioning script.

function.description: Run a process on this darwin machine
function.name: run_process

stdin_text.description: Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.

command_line.description: Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms

argv.description: Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.

function.description: Fetch a URL and extract HTML contents as markdown
function.name: fetch

raw.description: Do not convert HTML to markdown

max_chars.description: Maximum number of characters to return

start_index.description: Start at this character offset, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required

function.description: Delegate to a subagent to perform research and summarize findings.
function.name: delegate

agent_type.description: which subagent profile to use, options: general, web-researcher, command-runner

description.description: describe the task for the agent to perform

patch.description: file changes in custom diff format

code.description: Lua code to execute. Should be a valid Lua chunk.

function.name: run_lua
function.description: Execute arbitrary Lua code in the Neovim process. Useful for calling vim API functions like vim.notify or vim.cmd.

messages.content:
You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

Never modify files outside of the current working directory unless requested.

Current directory: ~/repos/github/g0t4/course-ansible-admin/lab
Repository root: ~/repos/github/g0t4/course-ansible-admin
You are 1 levels deep, so you need ../ to build relative paths from repo root.

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="gptoss120b<wes.mcclure+gptoss120b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.


## Fetch Tool

- Use repo's README `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/evals/refs/heads/main/README.md` instead of `https://github.com/openai/evals`.
- Prefer raw content like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g0t4/dotfiles/refs/heads/master/install/symlinks.fish` instead of `https://github.com/g0t4/dotfiles/blob/master/install/symlinks.fish`
- When relevant, prefer github CLI: `gh pr list` or `gh issue view 12`


## Use `apply_patch` to edit files.

Your patch language is a stripped‑down, file‑oriented diff format designed to be easy to parse and safe to apply.

*** Begin Patch
[ one or more file operations ]
*** End Patch

Each “operation” MUST start with one of three “headers”:

*** Add File: <path> - every following line is a + line (the initial contents).
*** Delete File: <path> - remove an existing file. Nothing follows.
*** Update File: <path> - in-place edits (optionally with a rename).

UpdateFile can be immediately followed by MoveTo to rename the file:
*** Move to: <new_path>

Then one or more “hunks”, each introduced by @@ (optionally followed by a hunk header).

Within a hunk each line starts with:
+ for inserted text,
- for removed text, or
  space (" ") for context (unchanged lines to match)

At the end of a truncated hunk you can emit:
*** End of File

### Here is the grammar:

Patch := Begin { FileOp } End
Begin := "*** Begin Patch" NEWLINE
End := "*** End Patch" NEWLINE
FileOp := AddFile | DeleteFile | UpdateFile
AddFile := "*** Add File: " path NEWLINE { "+" line NEWLINE }
DeleteFile := "*** Delete File: " path NEWLINE
UpdateFile := "*** Update File: " path NEWLINE [ MoveTo ] { Hunk }
MoveTo := "*** Move to: " newPath NEWLINE
Hunk := "@@" [ header ] NEWLINE { HunkLine } [ "*** End of File" NEWLINE ]
HunkLine := (" " | "-" | "+") text NEWLINE

### A full patch can combine several operations:

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: hello.txt
+Hello world
*** Update File: src/app.py
*** Move to: src/main.py
@@ def greet():
-print("Hi")
+print("Hello, world!")
*** Delete File: obsolete.txt
*** End Patch

### Reminders

- Verify your changes with `git diff foo.json`
- Nothing wrong with commands too:
  - `rm foo.json` to delete
  - `mv foo.json bar.json` to rename
  - `rename` for bulk rename
- Don't be that guy that uses DeleteFile followed by AddFile just to rename a file!


Tips on a failure:
- You only need enough context for a unique match!
- The line(s) you replace are context too; if they’re unique, no additional context is needed.
- Split bigger patches into smaller patches, i.e. if editing two functions, try each separately!
- Watch out for whitespace differences, i.e. blank lines

Please be curteous:
- Check your work!

### More Examples

Here's a patch file to change the `win` property's type:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@ @dataclass
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
-    gap_rms: TimeSeries
-    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
+    gap_rms: TimeSeries
+    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
*** End Patch
```

Which can be simplified to:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

Or, this could work if the `win` property is unique:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-    win: GapWindow
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

## General Code Preferences

- Avoid ambiguity, for example:

```lua
-- 👎 if there's a bug in the regex, how would I know? I'd have to surmise from surrounding code 🤮
if line:match("^%s*$") then
    -- ...
end
```

```lua
-- 🙌 clear intent
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

I want to change the current file ... create 3 ubuntu VMs too

FYI, here is my current buffer in Neovim. Use this as context for my request:
```Vagrantfile

# -*- mode: ruby -*-
# vi: set ft=ruby :

Vagrant.configure("2") do |config|
  config.vm.box = "wesdemos/arch-arm"
  config.vm.box_check_update = false

  (1..3).each do |number|
    name = "server#{number}"

    config.vm.define name do |node|
      node.vm.network 'public_network', ip: "192.168.6.10#{number}", netmask: "21"
      node.vm.network 'forwarded_port', id: 'ssh', host: "810#{number}", guest: "22"
      node.vm.hostname = name
    end
  end

  config.vm.define "ubuntu1" do |ubuntu|
    ubuntu.vm.box = "wesdemos/ubuntu2604-arm"
    ubuntu.vm.provision "shell", inline: <<-SHELL
      sudo apt update # broken links
      sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh ripgrep
    SHELL
  end

  # config.vm.provision "ansible" do |ansible|
  #   # run just this provisioner:
  #   #   vagrant provision --provision-with ansible
  #   #
  #   # * must install ansible on host (accessible to vagrant command)
  #   #   * NOTE vagrant provides inventory to ansible commands (i.e. when it executes ansible-playbook)
  #   #     thus the inventory.yml/inventory.ini are not necessary for `vagrant provision` demos...
  #   #     but, they inventory lists can be helpful to test a playbook w/o vagrant involved (often faster to run ansible-playbook directly and iterate on that and then leave `vagrant provision` as the "production" pathway)
  #   # * docs: https://developer.hashicorp.com/vagrant/docs/provisioning/ansible
  #   # * ansible provisioner src:
  #   #    options: https://github.com/hashicorp/vagrant/blob/0b7d460a/plugins/provisioners/ansible/config/base.rb#L14-L34
  #   ansible.playbook = "bootstrap/playbook.yml"
  #   # ansible.limit = "server2"
  #   # ansible.raw_arguments = [ "--check", "--diff" ]
  #   # ansible.raw_arguments = [ "-vvv" ]
  #   # for packages, --diff will show name+version per pkg (+added/-removed)
  #   # ansible.extra_vars = { packages: ["inetutils"] }
  # end

  config.vm.provider 'parallels' do |vb|
    # nest inside loops above for targeting specific nodes
    vb.memory = '8192'
    vb.cpus = 4
  end
 
  # PRN if disk space runs out => see course-rancher Vagranfile for script to expand
  #   OR, modify packer build where it sets 20G disk...
  #   REMOVE THIS IF no issues in lab setup preparation

  config.vm.provision 'shell', path: "./bootstrap/dotfiles.fish", privileged: false

end


```

messages.reasoning_content:
User wants to change current file ... create 3 ubuntu VMs too. So modify Vagrantfile: currently has loop for server1-3 (arch-arm). Need also three ubuntu VMs similar. Probably add loop or define three ubuntu1-3. Simplest: replicate block for ubuntu1-3. Add after existing ubuntu1 block. Use loop.

We'll replace the single ubuntu1 definition with a loop (1..3) defining ubuntu#{number} with box "wesdemos/ubuntu2604-arm". Also provision same shell. Implement.

Patch Vagrantfile.

function.arguments:
{
  "patch": "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Vagrantfile
@@
-  config.vm.define "ubuntu1" do |ubuntu|
-    ubuntu.vm.box = "wesdemos/ubuntu2604-arm"
-    ubuntu.vm.provision "shell", inline: <<-SHELL
-      sudo apt update # broken links
-      sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh ripgrep
-    SHELL
-  end
+  (1..3).each do |number|
+    name = "ubuntu#{number}"
+    config.vm.define name do |ubuntu|
+      ubuntu.vm.box = "wesdemos/ubuntu2604-arm"
+      ubuntu.vm.provision "shell", inline: <<-SHELL
+        sudo apt update # broken links
+        sudo apt install node-typescript npm nodejs zsh ripgrep
+      SHELL
+    end
+  end
*** End Patch
*** End Patch"
}
function.name: apply_patch
